[2019-06-14 18:31] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): That's going to be so much soak and you need to buy it all around the same time to get max value.  Fun to think about getting max value, but idk how interesting the games would actually be.
Start of turn effect of "gain a y for each y" brings into question whether these effects resolve simultaneously or if they go one at a time, I guess the former makes more sense, but we haven't had that effect before.
Click effect is neat that it turns aegis into matrix for a limited amount of time, but the destroy part doesn't do much and is just clutter.
Complexity strikes: multiple build completion times (0 and 2), build time>1, new type of resonator, new type of conditional and targeted click effect, supply >1.  Seems like a large burden to understand the unit.

[2019-06-23 10:21] velizar_ (Name): This introduces two new resources to the game's base set. Or possibly in the extra set - they can only appear all together (3 units per resource).
It's not a serious proposal, I just thought it would be fun to share.
The numbers are approximate, if you think something is imbalanced then I recommend swapping it with a balanced version of the unit in your head.

Any unused white ⚪ or black 🌑 resource is lost at the end of your turn.

WHITE
generator: costs 3 :Gold:, creates 2 structures; each structure has 2 :Health: and on :Click:: pay 1 :Gold: to generate 1 ⚪ 
unit 1: costs 2 :Gold: 1 ⚪; 2 :Fragile:, :Blocker:
unit 2: costs 4 :Gold: 1 ⚪; 4 :Health:; each turn creates a permanent prompt 1hp barrier; timed life: 10 :Lifespan: 

BLACK
generator: costs 1 :Gold:; 1 :Lifespan: activates on the turn after your next turn and gives you 1 🌑
unit 1: costs 3 :Gold: 1 🌑; 2 :Health:; :Prompt:, :Frontline:; 1 :Lifespan: dies on next turn and deals 3 :Attack:
unit 2: costs 4 :Gold: 1 🌑; 4 :Health:, 1 :Attack:; :Frontline:
[2019-06-23 10:21] velizar_ (Name): Commentary

WHITE
generator: Buying white and then not using it is more punishing than floating green, but less punishing than wasting red or blue.
unit 1: Similar to Infusion Grid - more cost-effective than walls, but not prompt.
unit 2: Timed life to prevent stalemate. The benefit of this over an attacker is that it denies absorb, and sometimes defense is stronger. I'm suspicious that this is a poor unit, because it's effectively just un-buying an opponent's attacker. Defense isn't very often different from offense.
overall: Intended to be more breach-proof than blue, but less than green. Usually defensive rather than offensive.

BLACK
generator: Requires planning a lot in advance, and also gives your opponent time to react, so you need to also take that into account.
unit 1: More cost-effective than a Rhino, but not by much - black has high cost due to inflation. Forces your opponent to have enough attack. Would force opponent to target it instead of other frontline units. (Seems too strong with these numbers?)
unit 2: Plain attacker with frontline. (Might be too strong for base set, unless I'm underestimating inflation; could be 3 :Health:)
overall: Frontline units, opponent usually has to adapt their plan when they see this being bought. Having more black spending options will make them stronger, and make unit 1 especially stronger.
[2019-06-23 10:30] velizar_ (Name): Supply would be 10 or 20 for each unit.
